sourceMovie.title: The Living Daylights
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Living Daylights (1987) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Skyfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Living Daylights clicked for you, Skyfall (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Living Daylights?
faq.answer: Try Skyfall, Casino Royale, Spectre—each shares DNA with The Living Daylights while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Living Daylights?
faq.answer: The Living Daylights (1987) is best described as Action, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Living Daylights part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
